fields.description:
## Context
Records rebar's security assurance model (FISMA Moderate target; certification issued from a single Gerrit-authenticated controlled environment, meaning process-followed + no-blocking-issues, NOT a correctness guarantee) and frames the identity/op-cert security assessment findings as five ACCEPTED limitations (L1 key-validity-rests-on-access-control, L2 no-transparency-log, L3 TOFU-authorship, L4 single-environment/no-threshold, L5 process-not-correctness), each with its bounding control, authority tier, and tracked deferred-hardening idea ticket. Documented as an ADR to inherit the numbered/cross-referenceable accepted/superseded lifecycle already used by ADRs 0044/0045. File: docs/adr/0046-security-posture-and-accepted-limitations.md.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/adr/0046-security-posture-and-accepted-limitations.md` exists with status Accepted.
- [ ] The ADR states the FISMA-Moderate assurance target and the meaning of certification (process-followed + no-blocking-issues, explicitly not a correctness guarantee).
- [ ] Each of the five findings (L1-L5) is framed as an accepted limitation carrying a bounding control and an authority tier (never as an unmitigated threat).
- [ ] The ADR includes a prior-art/benchmark section comparing against gittuf, TUF, Sigstore, in-toto/SLSA, RFC 8725, and NIST SP 800-218 (SSDF) / SP 800-53.
- [ ] Deferred-hardening items reference the tracked idea tickets 2422, 4e1d, and 5100.
- [ ] [operator-attested] The ADR lands on main via Gerrit (Verified +1 and LLM-Review +1).
